_comment: The Unbundler registry — curated decompositions of gated data products. Every claim here was researched 2026-07-06 (subscription-arbitrage study, four research lanes). Deterministic by design: legal/licensing claims must never be LLM-generated. verdict values: arbitragable | partially_arbitragable | not_arbitragable | legal_review_required.

products.product: Candid Foundation Directory (formerly Foundation Center / GuideStar)
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Parsing, entity-linking and search UX over IRS filings that are public domain. Candid's grant records, funder profiles and payout histories are overwhelmingly derived from Form 990/990-PF data the IRS publishes for free, plus self-reported profile enrichment.

free_upstreams.source: IRS Form 990/990-PF e-file XML + SOI extracts
IRS Exempt Organizations Business Master File

rebuild_recipe: Pull SOI extracts + BMF from IRS.gov/NCCS (the old AWS 990 bucket is dead since 2021 — do not use it)
Parse 990-PF Part I/X/XII/XIII for giving capacity, payout math, grant lists
Entity-resolve on EIN; join BMF for status/assets/NTEE classification
Serve per-lookup instead of per-seat
products.price_math: Incumbent: $2k–12k/yr per seat. Rebuilt: pennies of compute per foundation lookup; a $0.20–0.25 per-call price undercuts a seat by ~99% for occasional users.
legal_traps: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer API is CC BY-NC-ND — do NOT build a commercial product on it; go to the IRS/NCCS primary files
The SOI extract's qualifying-distributions column is unpopulated for ~93% of rows — payout-compliance claims must be conservative (signal, never determination)
products.diy_difficulty: moderate — the XML schemas are messy across vintages; field-population gaps require conservative handling

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Grant-matching workflow UX over the same public 990 family plus grants.gov listings (also free and public).
rebuild_recipe: Same 990 pipeline as the Candid decomposition
Add grants.gov API for live federal opportunities
Match on NTEE codes + past-grantee similarity
products.price_math: Incumbent ~$300–1,000/mo. Per-call funder lookups + opportunity matches at $0.20–0.50 serve the long tail the subscription locks out.
legal_traps: Same 990-family traps as Candid

products.typical_price: ~$212,000/yr for a research university (FOIA-obtained contract pricing); smaller sites pay five figures
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: A curated citation graph, author disambiguation, and journal metrics — over scholarly metadata that now exists openly. Universities are publicly cancelling (2025–26 cancellation wave) in favor of open infrastructure.

free_upstreams.source: Crossref metadata + retractions (incl. Retraction Watch data)

rebuild_recipe: Query OpenAlex for works/authors/institutions/venues + citation counts
Cross-check DOIs against Crossref; flag retractions
Ship per-query confidence flags — OpenAlex author disambiguation is weaker than WoS's hand curation; honesty beats false precision
products.price_math: Incumbent: ~$212k/yr site license. Rebuilt: OpenAlex is free; per-call citation lookups at $0.05–0.25 make the marginal query ~4 orders of magnitude cheaper.
legal_traps: None on the data (CC0). The trap is quality, not license: disclose disambiguation limits per query
products.diy_difficulty: low-to-moderate — excellent open APIs; the work is in honest quality flags

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Entity resolution and search over US import bills of lading that are public by statute — 19 CFR 103.31 makes vessel manifest data publicly available.

free_upstreams.access: CBP FTP/DVD distribution — heavier ingestion than an API

rebuild_recipe: Ingest CBP AMS manifest releases (bulk; plan real ETL)
Entity-resolve consignees/shippers (the true value-add — messy names)
Index by company, product description, ports; serve per-lookup
Proof of feasibility: ImportYeti serves this data free with a ~12-person team
products.price_math: Incumbent $12k+/yr. Per-shipment-history lookups at $0.25–1.00 serve diligence and sourcing use cases without the subscription.
legal_traps: IMPORTS ONLY: AES export records are NOT public — do not promise export data
Shippers can request confidentiality — some records are legitimately masked

our_coverage.vertical: TradePulse (enhancement planned — bills-of-lading ingestion is a heavier build)

products.diy_difficulty: high — bulk ingestion + entity resolution; the data is free but not turnkey

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Same CBP AMS public manifest data as Panjiva, cheaper UX tier.
rebuild_recipe: See the Panjiva decomposition — identical upstream
products.price_math: Per-lookup pricing beats the $1,500–10,800/yr subscription for anyone below daily-power-user volume.
legal_traps: Same as Panjiva: imports only; confidentiality-masked records exist

products.product: S&P Capital IQ Pro / Market Intelligence — US bank & depository data
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Cleaned ratios and peer analytics over regulatory filings every US bank must file publicly.

free_upstreams.source: FDIC BankFind Suite API (financials, ratios, structure)

rebuild_recipe: Query FDIC BankFind for institution financials + ratios
Compute capital-adequacy reads against the actual PCA thresholds (12 CFR 324.403) — deterministic math, no estimates
Frame consumer answers around 'is my bank safe' signals: capital tier, NPL trend, deposit mix
products.price_math: Incumbent ~$100k/yr. A per-bank health check at $0.25 is ~5 orders of magnitude cheaper for the single-question user.
legal_traps: None — federal regulatory data. Keep claims to reported figures and defined regulatory categories

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: For munis specifically: convenience. MSRB's EMMA publishes real-time trade prices and continuing disclosures for free — the terminal's muni edge is workflow, not exclusive data.

free_upstreams.access: emma.msrb.org — no official bulk API; programmatic access requires care

rebuild_recipe: Pull recent trades + disclosure events per CUSIP/issuer from EMMA's public interfaces
Surface continuing-disclosure red flags (draw notices, rating changes, defaults)
Scope honestly if programmatic access proves fragile — a disclosure-event feed alone still beats a $32k seat for the occasional muni question
products.price_math: One terminal seat = $32k/yr. Per-CUSIP lookups at $0.25 serve advisors and agents who touch munis occasionally.
legal_traps: HARD STOP: FINRA TRACE corporate-bond data is NOT free for commercial use — the muni arbitrage does NOT extend to corporates
The rest of the terminal (equities L2, chat, analytics) is licensed/proprietary — this decomposition covers ONLY the muni sleeve
products.diy_difficulty: moderate-to-high — EMMA has no official API; respect its terms

products.moat_type: mixed — holdings are public; the taxonomy/ratings are proprietary
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Fund holdings themselves are free: every registered fund files N-PORT with the SEC monthly (public quarterly). Morningstar's proprietary value is its category taxonomy, star ratings, and analyst layer — that part is NOT arbitragable.
rebuild_recipe: Resolve fund → CIK/series via EDGAR company search
Parse latest N-PORT: positions, weights, concentration
Do NOT clone the ratings/taxonomy — that is genuinely theirs
products.price_math: Holdings questions ('what does fund X actually hold?') per-call at $0.25 vs a $17.5k seat.
legal_traps: Never reproduce Morningstar categories/ratings — proprietary. Holdings facts are public domain
products.diy_difficulty: moderate — N-PORT XML is large but well-structured

products.moat_type: mixed — filings search is public-data; broker research + expert calls are licensed
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: The filings-search core rides on EDGAR full-text search, which the SEC provides free (efts.sec.gov). The licensed broker research and expert-call library are real proprietary content you cannot rebuild.
rebuild_recipe: Wrap EDGAR full-text search with phrase queries across form types
Add anomaly heuristics: NT filings, Item 4.01/4.02 8-Ks, going-concern language
Be honest that broker research/expert calls are out of scope
products.price_math: The 'who said X in a filing' workflow per-call at $0.20–0.25 vs $125k/yr.
legal_traps: SEC requests a descriptive User-Agent and ~10 req/s ceiling — comply

endpoints: anomaly + transcript-search endpoints in active build

products.diy_difficulty: low for search; moderate for good anomaly heuristics

products.moat_type: mixed — government labor data is free; scraped-profile panels are their (legally fraught) edge
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Two layers: (1) analytics over free government labor data — arbitragable; (2) a scraped professional-profile panel — NOT safely rebuildable (see traps).

free_upstreams.source: DOL LCA + PERM disclosure files (visa wages, employers)

rebuild_recipe: Wage benchmarking from OEWS; skills mapping from O*NET
H-1B/PERM employer wage red-flag checks from DOL disclosure files
Do NOT scrape job postings or profiles
products.price_math: Government-layer wage/visa analytics per-call at $0.10–0.50 vs $85k bulk.
legal_traps: hiQ v. LinkedIn ended in a $500k judgment and injunction — profile/posting scraping is not a safe foundation
O*NET requires attribution

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Aggregation of contract opportunities and awards that governments publish free, plus pre-RFP analyst forecasts (their genuine add).
rebuild_recipe: Search SAM.gov + USASpending per NAICS/agency/keyword
Award-history context per incumbent vendor
Global: add TED/AusTender for non-US tenders
Note: FPDS ATOM feed is being decommissioned — build on SAM/USASpending, not FPDS
products.price_math: Per-search at $0.10–0.50 vs $29k/yr — the long tail of small contractors is priced out of GovWin entirely.
legal_traps: None on US federal data

products.typical_price: $289 per single document; subscriptions in the tens of thousands
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Indexed FDA Form 483s and warning letters — documents FDA publishes in FOIA reading rooms and databases for free.

free_upstreams.source: FDA warning letters database + FOIA electronic reading room (483s)

rebuild_recipe: Index warning letters + posted 483s by firm/site/violation theme
Join openFDA inspection classifications
Serve per-firm regulatory-history checks
products.price_math: $289/document vs a $0.25 per-firm scan on the same public record.
legal_traps: Not all 483s are proactively posted — some require a FOIA request; be honest about coverage gaps

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Aggregation and charting of statistics that national agencies publish free: FRED, BLS, Eurostat, national central banks.

free_upstreams.source: BLS, BEA, Eurostat, ECB, national statistics offices

rebuild_recipe: Map user questions to the right free series
Serve computed reads (trends, regime context) per-call
products.price_math: Per-question macro reads at $0.05–0.25 vs $324–7,200/yr.
legal_traps: A few emerging-market series are genuinely gated at the source — coverage honesty required

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Normalized access to grid data the ISOs publish free: LMPs, load, fuel mix.
rebuild_recipe: Per-call LMP/load lookups against ISO public feeds
Normalize node/zone naming (the real work)
products.price_math: Per-lookup at $0.05–0.25 vs subscription tiers.
legal_traps: Some ISO real-time feeds have registration requirements — read each ISO's terms

endpoints: https://gridpulse.theaslangroupllc.com (energy intel live; per-call LMP enhancement planned)

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Workflow over free satellite data: the deforestation determination itself rides on open imagery.

free_upstreams.source: Global Forest Watch / Hansen forest-change data

rebuild_recipe: Plot-level forest-cover-change check against the EUDR cutoff date (2020-12-31)
Return evidence layers (imagery dates, change detection) suitable for a due-diligence statement
products.price_math: Per-plot checks at $0.50–2.00 vs $15k+ platform minimums — the deadline-driven long tail (small importers) is unserved.
legal_traps: Attribution required (CC-BY). Your check supports a due-diligence statement; it is not itself legal compliance advice
products.diy_difficulty: high — geospatial pipeline, but entirely open-source-able

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Aggregation of recall feeds that safety agencies publish free, globally.
rebuild_recipe: Join CPSC + openFDA + RASFF per product/brand/category
Per-check or SKU-watch pricing
products.price_math: Per-check at $0.05–0.25 vs $10k+/yr.
legal_traps: None material

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Batch UX over three free federal lists every provider can query directly.
rebuild_recipe: Resolve provider via NPI
Check LEIE + SAM exclusions
Return a combined per-provider verdict with source citations
products.price_math: Per-provider combo checks proven viable at $0.25–5.00 per call vs annual platform fees.
legal_traps: None — federal lists. Screening obligations/cadence are the customer's regulatory duty; you provide the check, not the compliance program

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Accident and service records sourced from exclusive commercial agreements with insurers, body shops, and dealers. That data does not exist in any public record you can rebuild from.

free_upstreams.access: vpic.nhtsa.dot.gov — decodes the vehicle, does NOT give history

rebuild_recipe: Cannot be rebuilt from public data. NMVTIS (title brands/salvage) is the closest public-interest dataset but requires becoming an AAMVA-approved provider (~$1k gate + approval)
products.price_math: n/a — the moat is the data itself, not the curation
legal_traps: NMVTIS access is gated behind AAMVA approved-provider status — not self-serve

our_coverage.vertical: AutoPulse covers the public layer (recalls, TCO, negotiation) — not accident history

products.diy_difficulty: n/a — this is the discriminating counter-example: when the moat is data that never touches a public record, there is no arbitrage

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Valuations, round sizes, and cap-table intel sourced from voluntary disclosure networks (Crunchbase's ~4,000-firm Venture Program) and analyst calls. Private-market financials are not public records.

free_upstreams.access: EDGAR — discloses that a raise happened and its size band, NOT valuation

rebuild_recipe: Only a thin Form D trigger feed is rebuildable ('company X just filed a raise'). Valuations/cap tables cannot be derived from public data — pre-IPO perp markets give implied marks for a handful of names only
products.price_math: n/a for the core product
legal_traps: Scraping their databases violates terms and their data is compiled, not public record

our_coverage.vertical: VenturePulse (Form D layer) + SignalPulse pre-ipo (perp-implied marks for traded names)

products.product: S&P Platts / Argus commodity price assessments
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: The assessments ARE the product: journalists/analysts canvass private OTC deals and publish an editorial price. There is no public record to rebuild it from, and the assessments are contractually embedded in physical-trade settlement worldwide.

free_upstreams.access: exchanges — a different product than physical assessments

rebuild_recipe: Cannot rebuild the assessment layer; futures-based proxies serve some use cases honestly labeled as such
products.price_math: n/a
legal_traps: Republishing assessments is licensed content infringement

products.moat_type: contested — codes are law, but SDOs claim copyright
products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Access to codes that are enacted into law (which courts have increasingly held cannot be copyrighted once incorporated by reference) — but the standards bodies litigate.

free_upstreams.source: Codes incorporated by reference into public law

rebuild_recipe: Do not build here without counsel: ASTM v. UpCodes is LIVE litigation (3rd Circuit, April 2026). The legal ground is actively moving
products.price_math: attractive but premature
legal_traps: Active litigation defines this category — legal review is mandatory before any build

our_coverage.vertical: none (deliberately skipped pending ASTM v. UpCodes)

products.what_you_actually_pay_for: Real-time short-interest ESTIMATES built from paid securities-lending data feeds. The free public record (FINRA/exchange short interest) is twice-monthly and lagged — the real-time layer is genuinely proprietary.
rebuild_recipe: The lagged public layer is rebuildable and cheap; the real-time estimate layer is not. In cheap-retail categories like this, the agent-native angle (per-call, no seat) is the edge — not price
products.price_math: public-layer lookups at $0.05–0.10; do not promise real-time
legal_traps: None on the public layer
products.diy_difficulty: low for the lagged layer; the real-time layer requires the same paid feeds
